betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§r[warn]This multiblock is always doing recipes on the same tier as the energy hatches in the structure. If the machine does not have enough power supplied, it won't turn on.[/warn]

Even though this multiblock is avalailable in IV we recommend building one §nafter finishing naqline§r§l


This multiblock has 3 different modes: §3Normal§r§r, §4EBF§r and §6Ore§r


In §3Normal§r mode this multiblock §3processes normal arc furnace recipes§r.

In §4EBF§r mode this multiblock can process §4every EBF recipe regardless of coil tier requirement§r. This feature comes at the cost of §lbase recipe voltage being increased by 2 tiers§r.

In §6Ore§r mode this machine§n§6 §ncan only process ores and raw§r§r ores §rthat can be smelted in normal furnace, giving molten metal directly.


In addition to the modes, this machine has 3 different processing phases

§§§lStart§lu§r§l§lp§r§r§l:§r§r§l§r§r §r§rDuring this phase the machine will use power equal to §lmaximum parrarels * number of amps per parrarel * 
(1+ startup penalty). §rThis phase lasts for 6 seconds before going to processing phase.

§lProcessing:§r During this phase the machine behaves as any other multiblock. Its power usage is determined by the Amp/Parrarel statistic on the electrode that is being used. If the machine fails to find a recipe that it can use it goes into shutdown phase.

§lShutdown:§r Machine enters a 6 second phase during which it does not process any recipes and does not use any power. After shutdown phase to start processing recipes you need to go through startup again.


Statistics of this multiblock are mostly dependant on electrode that is being used. This ranges from speed, parrarels, OC's and even ammount of Amps per parrarel.

If the used electrode drops below 30% durability there is 5% chance that the machine will have to go through startup phase again.

If the used electrode drops below 10% durability there is 2% chance that the machine will §lvoid ALL§r outputs
